1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	- archon-coordinator-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-coordinator-agent.py` — decompose a multi-part directive and dispatch children in parallel
24	- archon-review-*-agent: five read-only review subagents (`review-definition-correctness`, `review-comment-hygiene`, `review-blueprint-consistency`, `review-design-choices`, `review-mathlib-overlap`) that audit one aspect of the project each. Typically dispatched from the review phase.
25	
26	All subagent tools share the same argument shape: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path> [--write-domain '<glob>']...`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
27	
28	## Key Files & Permissions
29	
30	All state files are in `.archon/`:
31	
32	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
33	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
34	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
35	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
36	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
37	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
39	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
40	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
41	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
42	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
43	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
44	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
45	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
46	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
47	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
48	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
49	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
50	
51	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
52	
53	## Protected Declarations
54	
55	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
56	
57	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
58	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
59	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
60	
61	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
62	
63	The blueprint uses two active markers.
64	
65	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
66	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
67	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
68	
69	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
70	
71	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
72	
73	## User Interaction
74	
75	Users provide hints in two places:
76	
77	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
78	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
79	
80	## Agent Roles
81	
82	### Plan Agent
83	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
84	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
85	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
86	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
87	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
88	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
89	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
90	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
91	
92	### Prover Agent
93	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
94	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
95	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
96	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
97	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
98	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
99	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
100	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
101	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
102	
103	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
104	
105	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
106	
107	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
108	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
109	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
110	
111	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
112	
113	### Review Agent
114	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
115	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
116	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
117	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
118	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
119	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
120	
121	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
122	
123	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
124	
125	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
126	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
127	
128	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## LSP MCP Tools: Invocation Rules
73	
74	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls**, not shell commands. Their full names start with `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__` (e.g. `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_local_search`). The lean4 skill reference uses the short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) for readability — those are the **same tools**, never standalone shell binaries.
75	
76	- **Always invoke them through your tool-call interface**, the same way you call `Read` or `Grep`.
77	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` / `lean_diagnostic_messages …` — there is no such shell command and it will fail with `command not found`.
78	- As your **first LSP action**, call `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your assigned file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
79	
80	## Search Protocol
81	
82	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
83	
84	1. `lean_local_search` first
85	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
86	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
87	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
88	
89	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
90	
91	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
92	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
93	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
94	
95	## Tooling Traps & Verification
96	
97	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
98	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
99	
100	## Logging
101	
102	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
103	
104	**Format:**
105	
106	```markdown
107	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
108	
109	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
110	### Attempt 1
111	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
112	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
113	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
114	
115	### Attempt 2
116	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
117	- **Result:** RESOLVED
118	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
119	
120	## helper_bijective (line 78)
121	### Attempt 1
122	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
123	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
124	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
125	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
126	```
127	
128	**Rules:**
129	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
130	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
131	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
132	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
133	
134	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
135	
136	## Summary Pipeline
137	
138	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
139	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
140	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
141	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
142	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
143	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
144	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
145	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
146	
147	## End-of-session handoff
148	
149	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
150	
151	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
152	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
153	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
154	   - Concrete next step for the next session
155	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
156	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
157

26	## Big-picture context
27	
28	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
29	curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`).
30	The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables.
31	
32	See `STRATEGY.md` for the forward-looking arc. Phase A's blocker shape:
33	iter-093 = LinearMap.comp_apply eqToHom-mismatch; iter-094 = HOU on `key₂` /
34	`Preadditive.sum_comp`-application to nested-`i` summand body; iter-095
35	exhausted three tactic-level routes; iter-096 refactor added abstract
36	structural lemma; iter-097 closed Step 1 + exhausted 6 bridging routes for
37	Step 2; iter-098 refactor split the morphism slot into G+E; iter-099 closed
38	the new lemma's body AND applied it at the call site, narrowing the residual
39	to a per-summand `?hG` discharge; iter-100 funext pivot; iter-101 S1-S3
40	fuse + S4 exhausted; iter-102 refactor lanes (new σ-binder lemma inert,
41	call-site rewrite reverted due to 12800000 heartbeat timeout); iter-103
42	closed σ-binder lemma body via Path B (binder-level proof) but Path A on
43	L827 failed across 5 routes; iter-104 refactor lane

81	### Step 1 — close the L536 R-linearity body
82	
83	The body sorry at L536 is the *binder-level* R-linearity of the named
84	Čech coface morphism family. The proof is HOU-free because
85	`cechCofaceMap_summand_family` is a named top-level constant (not an
86	anonymous closure), so all discrimination-tree-friendly tactics
87	(`simp`, `rw`, `simp only`) work on its `Pi.lift` body via the standard
88	Pi.lift API (`Pi.lift_π_apply`, etc).
89	
90	**The goal at L536** (verified via `lean_goal`):
91	
92	```
93	⊢ ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
94	    e_int ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
95	      r • e_int ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i).hom (e₁.symm y))
96	```
97	
98	where:
99	- `R = Γ(C.left, U)`
100	- `Z₁ = fun (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀) ↦
101	       ModuleCat.of k ↑(C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i)))`
102	- `Z_int = fun (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀) ↦
103	          ModuleCat.of k ↑(C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i)))`
104	- `e₁ = (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv`
105	- `e_int = (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z_int).toLinearEquiv`
106	- The R-module structures on `∀ i, Z₁ i` and `∀ i, Z_int i` are Pi.module +
107	  RingHom.toModule (via `C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op`).
108	
109	The morphism `cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i` (unfolded) is:
110	
111	```
112	Pi.lift fun i_1 : Fin ((prev n) + 2) → ↑s₀ ↦
113	  Pi.π Z₁ (i_1 ∘ (δ i).toOrderHom) ≫
114	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift fun x ↦ Pi.π (...) ((δ i).toOrderHom x)).op
115	```
116	
117	**Mathematical structure** (~15-20 LOC at binder level). At output
118	coordinate `j' : Fin ((prev n) + 2) → ↑s₀`, the composite reads:
119	
120	```
121	((presheaf.map _).hom (y (j' ∘ (δ i).toOrderHom)))
122	```
123	
124	R-linearity follows from `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` (L425, fully
125	proved iter-087): the chain `R = Γ(C.left, U) → Γ(C.left,
126	V_{j'∘δ i}) → Γ(C.left, ?)` collapses, and the R-action on `Z_int j'`
127	is exactly the map `R → Γ(C.left, ∏ᶜ basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ j')`. The
128	restriction map preserves the R-action because of how `perI₁` and
129	`perI_int` are defined (same `RingHom.toModule (presheaf.map _.op).hom`
130	construction).
131	
132	**Recommended tactic chain (Path A)**:
133	
134	```lean
135	-- Step 1: introduce r, y; unfold the named family to its Pi.lift body.
136	intro r y
137	unfold cechCofaceMap_summand_family  -- or `dsimp [cechCofaceMap_summand_family]`
138	-- Goal now has Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ Pi.π Z₁ ... ≫ presheaf.map _ as the morphism.
139	
140	-- Step 2: apply LinearMap.ext + intro j' to pivot to per-coordinate goal.
141	apply LinearMap.ext  -- or use `funext j'` after suitable shape
142	intro j'
143	-- Per-coordinate goal: at output coord j',
144	--   e_int ((Pi.lift _).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j' = r • e_int ((Pi.lift _).hom (e₁.symm y)) j'
145	
146	-- Step 3: simp away the LinearEquiv coercions and Pi.lift_π.
147	simp only [LinearEquiv.coe_coe, ModuleCat.piIsoPi_apply, Pi.lift_π_apply,
148	  ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]
149	-- After simp: goal has shape
150	--   (presheaf.map _).hom (y (j' ∘ δ i)) `with r-prefix on LHS` 
151	-- or similar binder-level form.
152	
153	-- Step 4: discharge via presheafMap_restrict_collapse (per-coordinate).
154	-- The R-action on Z_int j' is RingHom.toModule (presheaf.map _.op).
155	-- (presheaf.map _).hom (r • _) = r • (presheaf.map _).hom _ by RingHom.toModule's
156	-- map_mul property — but we DON'T need this; we use the fact that
157	-- e₁.symm (r • y) = r • e₁.symm y (LinearEquiv preserves smul, by map_smul).
158	simp only [map_smul, LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply, ...]  -- adjust as needed
159	-- Per-coord: r • (presheaf.map _).hom (y_evaluated) commutes with r-action.
160	-- presheafMap_restrict_collapse (or direct) closes.
161	exact presheafMap_restrict_collapse _ _ _ _  -- adjust args
162	```
163	
164	(The exact tactic order and simp lemmas may need iteration. Use
165	`lean_goal` per step to check progress, and `lean_multi_attempt` for
166	unstable rewrites.)

168	**Fallback ladder**:
169	
170	- **(S1-F) `unfold` fails to unfold**: try `dsimp only [cechCofaceMap_summand_family]`
171	  or `change` with the explicit body shape. The body is reducibility-
172	  default so it SHOULD unfold; if not, `delta cechCofaceMap_summand_family`.
173	- **(S2-F) `LinearMap.ext` doesn't apply**: the goal is `e_int F (e₁.symm (r • y))
174	  = r • e_int F (e₁.symm y)`. The `e_int` and `e₁.symm` are LinearEquiv
175	  applications. Pivot via `LinearEquiv.eq_symm_apply` or `apply
176	  LinearEquiv.injective e_int.symm` first, or work at the LinearEquiv level
177	  via `LinearEquiv.map_smul`.
178	- **(S3-F) `Pi.lift_π_apply` doesn't fire**: check the exact form (some
179	  Mathlib versions need `Pi.lift_π` vs `Pi.lift_π_apply`; try
180	  `ConcreteCategory.comp_apply` + `Limits.Pi.lift_π` as separate steps).
181	- **(S4-F) `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` doesn't close**: it expects
182	  three opens `V ≤ W ≤ U` and proves `(presheaf.map h_VW).hom ((presheaf.map h_WU).hom r) = (presheaf.map h_VU).hom r`.
183	  If the goal is `R-linearity of (presheaf.map _).hom`, that's a different
184	  property — derive via `(presheaf.map _).hom` being a CommRingCat morphism
185	  hence R-linear (via `RingHom.toModule` from the same presheaf restriction).
186	
187	**Why this should work**: at the binder level, `Pi.lift_π_apply` reduces
188	the `Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` evaluated at `j'` to the body with `i_1 := j'`.
189	This is the EXACT step that failed inside `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`'s body
190	(L827) because the goal there had `Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ <body referencing
191	outer i>`. At THIS level, `i` is a named binder of the surrounding theorem,

479	/-- Iter-102 refactor (cechcoface-named-family): per-summand R-linearity of the
480	sign-free Čech coface component `cechCofaceMap_summand_family`. The R-action on
481	the domain (`∏ᶜ Z₁`-side) is the Pi.module structure with each factor's
482	restriction-algebra structure; the R-action on the codomain (`∏ᶜ Z_int`-side) is
483	similarly the Pi.module structure with each factor's restriction-algebra
484	structure. The morphism factors through
485	`Pi.π Z_pre (i_1 ∘ δ i) ≫ presheaf.map _.op`, so R-linearity follows per
486	coordinate from `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` + `Pi.lift_π_apply`.

489	1. `intro r y`.
490	2. `apply LinearMap.ext`; `intro j`.   [goal: per-coordinate R-linearity]
491	3. `simp only [Pi.lift_π_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ...]`.
492	4. The composite at output coord `j` reads `(presheaf.map _).hom
493	   (y (j ∘ δ i))`. R-linearity follows from `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`. -/
494	theorem cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear
495	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
496	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
497	    (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
498	    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n)
499	    (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2)) :
500	    let R := Γ(C.left, U)
501	    let Z₁ : (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀) → ModuleCat.{u} k :=
502	      fun i => ModuleCat.of k
503	        (C.left.presheaf.obj
504	          (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a))))
505	    let Z_int : (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀) → ModuleCat.{u} k :=
506	      fun i => ModuleCat.of k
507	        (C.left.presheaf.obj
508	          (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a))))
509	    letI perI₁ : ∀ i, Module R (Z₁ i) := fun i => by
510	      apply RingHom.toModule
511	      refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
512	      let a0 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
513	      have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
514	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
515	      have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
516	        basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
517	      exact h1.trans h2
518	    letI h_mod_pi₁ : Module R (∀ i, Z₁ i) := Pi.module _ _ _
519	    letI perI_int : ∀ i, Module R (Z_int i) := fun i => by
520	      apply RingHom.toModule
521	      refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
522	      let a0 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
523	      have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
524	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
525	      have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
526	        basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
527	      exact h1.trans h2
528	    letI h_mod_pi_int : Module R (∀ i, Z_int i) := Pi.module _ _ _
529	    let e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] (∀ i, Z₁ i) :=
530	      (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv
531	    let e_int : (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] (∀ i, Z_int i) :=
532	      (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z_int).toLinearEquiv
533	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
534	      e_int ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
535	        r • e_int ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
536	  sorry

539	/-- Iter-096 (refactor extraction): structural lemma — if each summand
540	`F i` is R-linear (with respect to the LinearEquivs `e₁`, `e₂`), then
541	so is `∑ i ∈ s, F i`. The summand family `F : ι' → (M ⟶ N)` is named
542	abstractly, so applying this lemma to the Čech alternating sum
543	`∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` only requires Miller-pattern
544	unification `F := fun i ↦ <literal summand>` — no HOU on the
545	nested-i body.
546	
547	Body left as `sorry` for the iter-097 prover. Proof sketch:
548	induct on the finset `s` via `Finset.cons_induction`. Base case is
549	the empty sum = 0, which is trivially R-linear via the `ModuleCat`
550	zero morphism. Cons step: `F i + ∑ j ∈ s', F j` is R-linear because
551	R-linearity is preserved under addition (`ModuleCat.hom_add` plus
552	R-linearity of each summand).
553	
554	**Refactor note (iter-096):** The directive also requested a companion
555	`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul_summand` that bakes the Čech summand literal
556	into its signature. That signature triggers a whnf timeout at the
557	`Pi.lift (fun x ↦ Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1) ((SimplexCategory.δ i)
558	.toOrderHom x))`-elaboration step, because Lean attempts to unify
559	`Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀` with `Fin n → ↥s₀` through
560	the heavy `ComplexShape.prev` definition. We omit that helper in iter-096;
561	the abstract structural lemma below suffices to give the iter-097 prover a
562	HOU-friendly hook (instantiate `F := fun i ↦ <Čech summand>` and discharge
563	per-summand R-linearity inline). -/
564	theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux
565	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
566	    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
567	    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u} (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
568	    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
569	    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
570	    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
571	    (F : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
572	    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
573	    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
574	    (hF : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
575	      e₂ ((F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
576	        r • e₂ ((F i).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
577	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
578	      e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
579	        r • e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, F i).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
580	  -- iter-097 Step 1: induct on `s` via `Finset.cons_induction`. Empty case
581	  -- collapses via `ModuleCat.hom_zero`; cons step distributes `Finset.sum_cons`
582	  -- + `ModuleCat.hom_add` + `LinearMap.add_apply` + `map_add` simultaneously
583	  -- on both sides via `simp only`, then applies `hF` for the head and `ih`
584	  -- for the tail, finishing with `smul_add`. HOU-free because `F` is a binder.
585	  revert hF
586	  induction s using Finset.cons_induction with
587	  | empty =>
588	    intro _hF r y
589	    simp [Finset.sum_empty, ModuleCat.hom_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply,
590	      map_zero, smul_zero]
591	  | cons i s' hi ih =>
592	    intro hF r y
593	    simp only [Finset.sum_cons, ModuleCat.hom_add, LinearMap.add_apply, map_add]
594	    rw [hF i (Finset.mem_cons_self i s')]
595	    rw [ih (fun j hj => hF j (Finset.mem_cons.mpr (Or.inr hj))) r y]
596	    rw [smul_add]

37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.

400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology
411	
412	/-- Iter-087 (refactor): per-summand R-linear restriction-collapse. For nested
413	opens `V ≤ W ≤ U` in a scheme `C.left`, the chain of presheaf restrictions
414	`Γ(U) → Γ(W) → Γ(V)` collapses to the direct restriction `Γ(U) → Γ(V)`.
415	
416	Used in the proof of R-linearity of the Čech differential
417	(`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`): each per-summand restriction chain
418	`R = Γ(C.left, U) → Γ(C.left, V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(C.left, V_j)` collapses to the
419	direct map `R → Γ(C.left, V_j)`, matching the per-i R-action induced by the
420	restriction algebra structure on the cochain factor.
421	
422	Extracted iter-087 from the inline `have R_restrict_R_linear` (iter-086) inside
423	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`. The body is the
424	2-tactic proof verified by the iter-086 prover. -/
425	lemma presheafMap_restrict_collapse
426	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
427	    {V W U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
428	    (h_VW : V ≤ W) (h_VU : V ≤ U) (h_WU : W ≤ U)
429	    (r : C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U)) :
430	    (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VW).op).hom
431	        ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_WU).op).hom r) =
432	      (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VU).op).hom r := by
433	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
434	  congr 1

436	/-- Iter-102 refactor (cechcoface-named-family): extracted top-level family for the
437	sign-free Čech coface morphism components. The body is the `Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...`
438	expression that appears (anonymously) in the post-iter-099 expanded
439	Čech differential inside `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`.
440	
441	Naming this family makes the head symbol a defined constant rather than
442	an anonymous closure, which unblocks the iter-099 `_sum_comp` /
443	`alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` call-site application:
444	- Miller unification on the G slot becomes trivial (G is given explicitly).
445	- The per-summand R-linearity hypothesis is a separate top-level theorem
446	  (`cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` below) that proves R-linearity
447	  of the named family at the binder level (HOU-free).
448	
449	The codomain index `Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀` keeps the
450	definition independent of `hn : 0 < n` — it is the natural domain of
451	`(SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom` for `i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2)`.
452	The call site bridges to `Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀` via the existing `eqToHom`
453	slot fed into `E` of `alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`. -/
454	noncomputable def cechCofaceMap_summand_family
455	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
456	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
457	    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) (n : ℕ)
458	    (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2)) :
459	    (∏ᶜ fun j : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀ =>
460	        ModuleCat.of k
461	          (C.left.presheaf.obj
462	            (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j a))))) ⟶
463	    (∏ᶜ fun j : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀ =>
464	        ModuleCat.of k
465	          (C.left.presheaf.obj
466	            (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j a))))) :=
467	  Pi.lift fun i_1 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀ =>
468	    Pi.π (fun j : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀ =>
469	            ModuleCat.of k
470	              (C.left.presheaf.obj
471	                (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j a)))))
472	        (i_1 ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫
473	      (toModuleKPresheaf C).map
474	        (Pi.lift fun x : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) =>
475	            Pi.π (fun a : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) =>
476	                    basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i_1 a))
477	              ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom x)).op

479	/-- Iter-102 refactor (cechcoface-named-family): per-summand R-linearity of the
480	sign-free Čech coface component `cechCofaceMap_summand_family`. The R-action on
481	the domain (`∏ᶜ Z₁`-side) is the Pi.module structure with each factor's
482	restriction-algebra structure; the R-action on the codomain (`∏ᶜ Z_int`-side) is
483	similarly the Pi.module structure with each factor's restriction-algebra
484	structure. The morphism factors through
485	`Pi.π Z_pre (i_1 ∘ δ i) ≫ presheaf.map _.op`, so R-linearity follows per
486	coordinate from `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` + `Pi.lift_π_apply`.
487	
488	Body left as `sorry` for the iter-105 prover. Proof sketch (~15 LOC):
489	1. `intro r y`.
490	2. `apply LinearMap.ext`; `intro j`.   [goal: per-coordinate R-linearity]
491	3. `simp only [Pi.lift_π_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ...]`.
492	4. The composite at output coord `j` reads `(presheaf.map _).hom
493	   (y (j ∘ δ i))`. R-linearity follows from `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`. -/
494	theorem cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear
495	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
496	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
497	    (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
498	    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n)
499	    (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2)) :

500	    let R := Γ(C.left, U)
501	    let Z₁ : (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀) → ModuleCat.{u} k :=
502	      fun i => ModuleCat.of k
503	        (C.left.presheaf.obj
504	          (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a))))
505	    let Z_int : (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀) → ModuleCat.{u} k :=
506	      fun i => ModuleCat.of k
507	        (C.left.presheaf.obj
508	          (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a))))
509	    letI perI₁ : ∀ i, Module R (Z₁ i) := fun i => by
510	      apply RingHom.toModule
511	      refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
512	      let a0 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
513	      have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
514	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
515	      have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
516	        basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
517	      exact h1.trans h2
518	    letI h_mod_pi₁ : Module R (∀ i, Z₁ i) := Pi.module _ _ _
519	    letI perI_int : ∀ i, Module R (Z_int i) := fun i => by
520	      apply RingHom.toModule
521	      refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
522	      let a0 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
523	      have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
524	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
525	      have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
526	        basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
527	      exact h1.trans h2
528	    letI h_mod_pi_int : Module R (∀ i, Z_int i) := Pi.module _ _ _
529	    let e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] (∀ i, Z₁ i) :=
530	      (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv
531	    let e_int : (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] (∀ i, Z_int i) :=
532	      (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z_int).toLinearEquiv
533	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
534	      e_int ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
535	        r • e_int ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n i).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
536	  sorry

539	/-- Iter-096 (refactor extraction): structural lemma — if each summand
540	`F i` is R-linear (with respect to the LinearEquivs `e₁`, `e₂`), then
541	so is `∑ i ∈ s, F i`. The summand family `F : ι' → (M ⟶ N)` is named
542	abstractly, so applying this lemma to the Čech alternating sum
543	`∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` only requires Miller-pattern
544	unification `F := fun i ↦ <literal summand>` — no HOU on the
545	nested-i body.
546	
547	Body left as `sorry` for the iter-097 prover. Proof sketch:
548	induct on the finset `s` via `Finset.cons_induction`. Base case is
549	the empty sum = 0, which is trivially R-linear via the `ModuleCat`
550	zero morphism. Cons step: `F i + ∑ j ∈ s', F j` is R-linear because
551	R-linearity is preserved under addition (`ModuleCat.hom_add` plus
552	R-linearity of each summand).
553	
554	**Refactor note (iter-096):** The directive also requested a companion
555	`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul_summand` that bakes the Čech summand literal
556	into its signature. That signature triggers a whnf timeout at the
557	`Pi.lift (fun x ↦ Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1) ((SimplexCategory.δ i)
558	.toOrderHom x))`-elaboration step, because Lean attempts to unify
559	`Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀` with `Fin n → ↥s₀` through
560	the heavy `ComplexShape.prev` definition. We omit that helper in iter-096;
561	the abstract structural lemma below suffices to give the iter-097 prover a
562	HOU-friendly hook (instantiate `F := fun i ↦ <Čech summand>` and discharge
563	per-summand R-linearity inline). -/
564	theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux
565	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
566	    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
567	    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u} (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
568	    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
569	    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
570	    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
571	    (F : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
572	    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
573	    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
574	    (hF : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
575	      e₂ ((F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
576	        r • e₂ ((F i).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
577	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
578	      e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
579	        r • e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, F i).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
580	  -- iter-097 Step 1: induct on `s` via `Finset.cons_induction`. Empty case
581	  -- collapses via `ModuleCat.hom_zero`; cons step distributes `Finset.sum_cons`
582	  -- + `ModuleCat.hom_add` + `LinearMap.add_apply` + `map_add` simultaneously
583	  -- on both sides via `simp only`, then applies `hF` for the head and `ih`
584	  -- for the tail, finishing with `smul_add`. HOU-free because `F` is a binder.
585	  revert hF
586	  induction s using Finset.cons_induction with
587	  | empty =>
588	    intro _hF r y
589	    simp [Finset.sum_empty, ModuleCat.hom_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply,
590	      map_zero, smul_zero]
591	  | cons i s' hi ih =>
592	    intro hF r y
593	    simp only [Finset.sum_cons, ModuleCat.hom_add, LinearMap.add_apply, map_add]
594	    rw [hF i (Finset.mem_cons_self i s')]
595	    rw [ih (fun j hj => hF j (Finset.mem_cons.mpr (Or.inr hj))) r y]
596	    rw [smul_add]
597	
598	/-- Iter-098 refactor: sum-through-composition extension of
599	`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux`. The existing lemma's single morphism

600	family `F : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂))` cannot absorb an intermediate
601	`eqToHom` because instantiating `F := fun i ↦ G i ≫ eqToHom`
602	re-introduces a `Fin (...) → ↑s₀` vs `Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀` unification
603	problem outside tactic state (the iter-097 Attempt 5 dead-end). This
604	parallel lemma splits the morphism into a family `G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶
605	(∏ᶜ Z_int))` plus a single `E : (∏ᶜ Z_int) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂)`, so the

610	`rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]` to convert
611	`(∑ G i) ≫ E` to `∑ (G i ≫ E)`, then `exact
612	alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂ hG r y`.

615	theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp
616	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
617	    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
618	    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι_int : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u}
619	    (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
620	    (Z_int : ι_int → ModuleCat.{u} k)
621	    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
622	    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
623	    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
624	    (G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
625	    (E : (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k))
626	    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
627	    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
628	    (hG : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
629	      e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
630	        r • e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
631	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
632	      e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
633	        r • e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
634	  -- iter-099 Step 1: distribute `Preadditive.sum_comp` (HOU-free since `G`
635	  -- and `E` are lemma binders, not literals), then apply
636	  -- `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` on the family `fun i ↦ G i ≫ E`.
637	  intro r y
638	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]
639	  exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂ hG r y

641	/-- Iter-102 refactor: zsmul-through-composition extension of
642	`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`. Adds a per-summand sign
643	`σ : ι' → ℤ` as a separate binder so that the per-summand R-linearity
644	hypothesis `hG` is about the **sign-free** composite `G i ≫ E` rather than
645	the sign-wrapped `(σ i • G i) ≫ E`. The body internally handles the
646	sign-to-R-action commutativity at the binder level, where Lean's
647	discrimination tree has no anonymous-closure to choke on.
648	
649	This unblocks the `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` `?hG` discharge: the iter-099
650	call site bakes `(-1)^↑i` into `?G`, leading to a discharge that requires
651	`Preadditive.zsmul_comp` on a Pi.lift with anonymous-closure codomain —
652	three consecutive prover lanes (iter-099, iter-100, iter-101) failed on
653	this class. With the new lemma, the discharge is about the sign-free
654	`Pi.lift_thing_i ≫ eqToHom`, which is closed via
655	`presheafMap_restrict_collapse` per coordinate.
656	
657	Body left as `sorry` for the iter-103 prover. Proof sketch (~5-10 lines):
658	1. `intro r y`.
659	2. `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s (fun i ↦ σ i • G i) E]` to distribute
660	   composition through the sum. The pattern `?f ≫ ?g` with `?f := σ i • G i`
661	   is HOU-free because the *family* `fun i ↦ σ i • G i` is a binder
662	   structurally identical to the family `G` in the iter-099 `_sum_comp`
663	   body.
664	3. `simp_rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` to pull each `σ i • _` outside the
665	   composition. **HOU-free** because `G` is a binder; `Preadditive.zsmul_comp`
666	   pattern `(?n • ?f) ≫ ?g` matches with `?f := G i` (typed variable).
667	4. Apply `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` with `F i := σ i • (G i ≫ E)` and
668	   a derived per-summand hypothesis `hF` proved inline from `hG` using
669	   `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul` + `smul_comm` + `hG`. All three steps work at
670	   the binder level.
671	-/
672	theorem alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp
673	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
674	    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
675	    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι_int : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u}
676	    (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
677	    (Z_int : ι_int → ModuleCat.{u} k)
678	    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
679	    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
680	    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
681	    (σ : ι' → ℤ)
682	    (G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
683	    (E : (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k))
684	    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
685	    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
686	    (hG : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
687	      e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
688	        r • e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
689	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
690	      e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, σ i • G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
691	        r • e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, σ i • G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
692	  -- iter-103 Path B: reduce to `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` on the family
693	  -- `fun i ↦ σ i • (G i ≫ E)` (single morphism per `i`, no nested closure).
694	  -- HOU-free because `G`, `E`, `σ` are binder-level typed variables.
695	  intro r y
696	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s (fun i ↦ σ i • G i) E]
697	  simp_rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]
698	  -- Goal: e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, σ i • (G i ≫ E)).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
699	  --       r • e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, σ i • (G i ≫ E)).hom (e₁.symm y))
700	  -- Use `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` with the per-summand R-linearity derived
701	  -- from `hG` by extracting `σ i` def-equally and `smul_comm`-ing with `r`.
702	  exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ σ i • (G i ≫ E)) e₁ e₂
703	    (fun i hi r y => by
704	      -- (σ i • (G i ≫ E)).hom = σ i • (G i ≫ E).hom by ModuleCat.hom_zsmul (rfl)
705	      -- and (σ i • L) z = σ i • L z by LinearMap.smul_apply (rfl). Both rfl at
706	      -- the BINDER LEVEL since (G i ≫ E) is a typed variable (no closure).
707	      show e₂ (σ i • (G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
708	        r • e₂ (σ i • (G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))
709	      rw [map_zsmul e₂ (σ i), map_zsmul e₂ (σ i),
710	          hG i hi r y, smul_comm (σ i) r])
711	    r y

714	-- `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` body's let-block reconstruction + S6 step (a)
715	-- `have hom_sum_dist` block + body-local key₁/key₂ helpers exceed the
716	-- default budget at the theorem head.
717	-- Iter-102 NOTE: the attempted use of `alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`
718	-- at the L773 call site forced a transient 8x heartbeat bump (12800000)
719	-- that still timed out at whnf; the call site was reverted to use
720	-- `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` and the budget restored to 1600000.
721	-- The sister lemma remains available as inert infrastructure for iter-103.

723	/-- Iter-087 (refactor, CORRECTIVE): R-linearity of the Čech-cochain
724	differential at degree `(prev n, n)` in the product representation, specialized
725	to the project's Čech-cochain context (`basicOpenCover` of an affine `U` from a
726	spanning finset `s₀`).
727	
728	**Statement specialization.** The previous iter-087 abstraction quantified
729	universally over `(Z₁, Z₂, e₁, e₂, scK₀_f, h_mod_pi₁, h_mod_pi₂, R)`, yielding
730	a mathematically false statement for arbitrary `scK₀_f`. This corrective
731	version fixes `R`, `K₀`, `scK₀`, `Z_i`, `e_i`, `h_mod_pi_i` from the
732	Čech-cochain context, so the conclusion is mathematically true: the Čech
733	differential is a finite alternating sum of presheaf restriction maps, each
734	R-linear (see `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`), hence the sum is R-linear
735	componentwise.
736	
737	The body is `sorry`; the iter-088+ prover closes it. The duplicated `letI`

938	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
939	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
940	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
941	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
942	schemes in our setting.
943	
944	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
945	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
946	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
947	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
948	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
949	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
950	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
951	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
952	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
953	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
954	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
955	
956	The structural skeleton is:
957	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
958	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
959	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
960	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
961	
962	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
963	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
964	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
965	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
966	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
967	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
968	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
969	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
970	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
971	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
972	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
973	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
974	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
975	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
976	
977	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
978	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/

101	
102	Given that for every affine open `U` of `C.left` there exists a spanning subset
103	`s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` with `Ideal.span s = ⊤` such that the basic-open cover
104	`basicOpenCover s : s → Opens` carries both `IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s)`
105	and `HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)`, conclude `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F`.
106	
107	Iter-055 specialises iter-053's existence-bundled cover form to the basic-open cover
108	shape constructed iter-054. Strategic value: factors out the existence-bundling step
109	from iter-056+ substantive work, so the iter-056+ Koszul acyclicity branch and the
110	iter-057+ Čech-vs-derived comparison branch can each prove specifically the basic-open
111	form (which the universal Koszul + comparison arguments naturally produce), and the
112	iter-055 producer then bundles their results into iter-053's carrier shape automatically.
113	
114	Body: destructures the per-affine existence to obtain `(s, hs, hacy, hcomp)`, then
115	bundles them into iter-053's existential cover via iter-054's
116	`basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top` to produce the supremum hypothesis. -/

120	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
121	    (h : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}, IsAffineOpen U →
122	        ∃ (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (_ : Ideal.span s = ⊤),
123	          IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s) ∧
124	          HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)) :

126	  exists_cover {U} hU := by
127	    obtain ⟨s, hs, hacy, hcomp⟩ := h hU
128	    exact ⟨s, basicOpenCover s, basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top hU s hs, hacy, hcomp⟩

150	
151	Given a Set `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` and indices `i j : s`, the intersection of the
152	two basic-open cover members `basicOpenCover s i` and `basicOpenCover s j`
153	equals the basic open of the product `i.1 * j.1`. Direct iteration of Mathlib's
154	`Scheme.basicOpen_mul`.
155	
156	This is the foundational identification for iter-057+ Čech complex evaluation:
157	the Čech cochain at degree 1 indexes pairs `(i, j) : s × s` and assigns the
158	sheaf evaluated at `basicOpenCover s i ⊓ basicOpenCover s j`. Via this lemma,
159	that intersection is identified with `C.left.basicOpen (i.1 * j.1)`, which by
160	`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (Mathlib) is the localization
161	`Localization.Away (i.1 * j.1)` of `Γ(C.left, U)`. The Koszul-style
162	alternating-sum exactness on these localizations follows from
163	`exact_of_localized_span` (Mathlib) under `Ideal.span s = ⊤`. -/
164	theorem basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul
165	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
166	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
167	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i j : s) :
168	    basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i ⊓ basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s j =
169	      C.left.basicOpen ((i.1 : Γ(C.left, U)) * j.1) := by
170	  unfold basicOpenCover
171	  exact (Scheme.basicOpen_mul C.left _ _).symm
172	
173	/-- Iter-056: pairwise basic-open intersection on an affine open is itself
174	affine.
175	
176	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`, `(s : Set Γ(C.left, U))`, and indices `i j : s`,
177	the intersection `basicOpenCover s i ⊓ basicOpenCover s j` is itself affine.
178	Thin wrapper combining `basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul` (rewriting the
179	intersection as a basic open of the product) with Mathlib's
180	`IsAffineOpen.basicOpen` (membership preservation under basic-open formation).
181	
182	Generalises iter-054's `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen` (single-index affine
183	membership) to pairwise intersections. Used by iter-057+ Čech evaluation: each
184	finite intersection of basic opens is itself affine, which is the inductive
185	step for the Koszul alternating-sum argument (Stacks 03F7 / Hartshorne III.4.5
186	/ Eisenbud Commutative Algebra §17 Koszul resolution). -/
187	theorem basicOpenCover_inter_isAffineOpen
188	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
189	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
190	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i j : s) :
191	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i ⊓
192	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s j) := by
193	  unfold basicOpenCover
194	  rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]
195	  exact hU.basicOpen _
196	
197	/-- Iter-057: n-ary basic-open intersection identification (Finset.inf' form).
198	
199	Given a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`, the infimum (over `t`) of the basic-open
200	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` equals the basic open of the
201	finite product `∏ i ∈ t, i.1` in `Γ(C.left, U)`. Generalises iter-056's binary
202	`basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul` to arbitrary non-empty Finset indexing.
203	
204	Body via `Finset.cons_induction`: the empty case is impossible by `t.Nonempty`
205	(closed via `Finset.not_nonempty_empty`); the cons case splits on whether the
206	residual `t` is empty or non-empty (via `Finset.eq_empty_or_nonempty`). In the
207	empty residual case, `simp [basicOpenCover]` closes via single-element reduction
208	of `Finset.inf'` and `Finset.prod`. In the non-empty residual case, decompose
209	via `Finset.inf'_cons hne` to expose `basicOpenCover s a ⊓ rest`, decompose
210	`Finset.prod_cons` to expose `a.1 * (∏ i ∈ t, i.1)`, apply the IH to identify
211	`rest = C.left.basicOpen (∏ i ∈ t, i.1)`, then `change ... ⊓ _ = _` followed by
212	`rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]` consolidates the product form.
213	
214	This is the foundational identification for iter-058+ Čech complex evaluation
215	at arbitrary degree `n`: the Čech cochain at degree `n` indexes `x : Fin (n+1) → s`
216	and assigns the sheaf evaluated at `⨅ i : Fin (n+1), basicOpenCover s (x i)`.
217	Via this lemma (instantiated at `t := (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (n+1))).image x`,
218	or more directly via `Finset.univ.inf' Finset.univ_nonempty (basicOpenCover s ∘ x)`),
219	that intersection identifies with `C.left.basicOpen (∏ i : Fin (n+1), (x i).1)`,
220	which by `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (Mathlib) is the
221	localization `Localization.Away (∏ i, (x i).1)` of `Γ(C.left, U)`. -/
222	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod
223	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
224	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
225	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
226	    t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) =
227	      C.left.basicOpen (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U))) := by
228	  classical
229	  induction t using Finset.cons_induction with
230	  | empty => exact absurd h Finset.not_nonempty_empty
231	  | cons a t ha ih =>
232	    rcases t.eq_empty_or_nonempty with rfl | hne
233	    · simp [basicOpenCover]
234	    · rw [Finset.inf'_cons hne, Finset.prod_cons, ih hne]
235	      change C.left.basicOpen (a.1 : Γ(C.left, U)) ⊓ _ = _
236	      rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]
237	
238	/-- Iter-057: n-ary basic-open intersection on an affine open is itself affine.
239	
240	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`, `(s : Set Γ(C.left, U))`, `(t : Finset s)`, and
241	`(h : t.Nonempty)`, the n-ary intersection `t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)` is
242	itself affine. Term-mode body chains
243	`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod` (rewriting the n-ary intersection
244	as a basic open of the finite product) with Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
245	(membership preservation under basic-open formation).
246	
247	Generalises iter-056's `basicOpenCover_inter_isAffineOpen` (pairwise affine
248	membership) to arbitrary non-empty Finset indexing. Used by iter-058+ Čech
249	evaluation: each finite intersection of basic opens of an affine is itself
250	affine, which is the inductive step for the Koszul alternating-sum argument
251	(Stacks 03F7 / Hartshorne III.4.5 / Eisenbud Commutative Algebra §17 Koszul
252	resolution).
253	
254	**Body uses `▸` (Eq.mpr), NOT `rw`**: `rw` on the equation produced by Theorem
255	(1) fails because of motive-occurrence issues with the implicit `Subtype.val`
256	coercions on elements of `Finset s`; term-mode `▸` rewrite resolves these
257	directly without invoking the rewriter's pattern-matching. Plan-agent
258	`lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirmed the term-mode form compiles
259	kernel-only while the tactic-mode `rw` form fails with "Did not find an
260	occurrence of the pattern" error. -/
261	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen
262	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
263	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
264	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
265	    IsAffineOpen (t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) :=
266	  basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod s t h ▸ hU.basicOpen _
267	
268	/-- Iter-058: n-ary basic-open intersection is contained in `U`.
269	
270	Given a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`, the infimum (over `t`) of the basic-open
271	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` is contained in `U`. Term-mode
272	body chains iter-057's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod` (rewriting
273	the n-ary intersection as a single basic open of the finite product) with
274	Mathlib's `Scheme.basicOpen_le` (`X.basicOpen f ≤ U` for any `f ∈ Γ(X, U)`)
275	via Lean's `▸` (Eq.mpr) rewrite.
276	
277	**No `IsAffineOpen` hypothesis required**: `basicOpen_le` holds for any open `U`
278	(the basic open of `f ∈ Γ(X, U)` is always a sub-open of `U`); compare iter-057's
279	`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen` which does need `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`.
280	
281	This is the inclusion morphism iter-059+ Čech-evaluation arguments need when
282	applying Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`:
283	```
284	(hU : IsAffineOpen U) (f : Γ(X, U)) {V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) :
285	  V = X.basicOpen f → IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, V)
286	```
287	The morphism `i : V ⟶ U` argument (which is `homOfLE` of `V ≤ U`) is needed to
288	install the `Algebra Γ(X, U) Γ(X, V)` instance for the conclusion type, since
289	Mathlib's `algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance only fires for the
290	syntactic form `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`, not for `V = t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)`.
291	Iter-058's inclusion lemma supplies this `V ≤ U` proof so iter-059+ can call
292	`hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen f (homOfLE (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le ...))
293	(basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod ...)` to land
294	`IsLocalization.Away (∏ i ∈ t, i.1) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s))`
295	directly. -/
296	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le
297	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
298	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
299	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :

.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Products.lean:63:theorem piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype (i : ι) :

/-- The categorical product of a family of objects in `ModuleCat`
agrees with the usual module-theoretical product.
-/
noncomputable def piIsoPi : ∏ᶜ Z ≅ ModuleCat.of R (∀ i, Z i) :=
  limit.isoLimitCone ⟨_, productConeIsLimit Z⟩

-- We now show this isomorphism commutes with the inclusion of the kernel into the source.
@[simp, elementwise]
theorem piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι (i : ι) :
    (piIsoPi Z).inv ≫ Pi.π Z i = ofHom (LinearMap.proj i : (∀ i : ι, Z i) →ₗ[R] Z i) :=
  limit.isoLimitCone_inv_π _ _

@[simp, elementwise]
theorem piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype (i : ι) :
    (piIsoPi Z).hom ≫ ofHom (LinearMap.proj i : (∀ i : ι, Z i) →ₗ[R] Z i) = Pi.π Z i :=
  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_inv_comp _ (limit.isLimit _) (Discrete.mk i)

end product

section coproduct

open DirectSum

variable [DecidableEq ι]

/-- The coproduct cone induced by the concrete coproduct. -/
def coproductCocone : Cofan Z :=
  Cofan.mk (ModuleCat.of R (⨁ i : ι, Z i)) fun i => ofHom (DirectSum.lof R ι (fun i ↦ Z i) i)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The concrete coproduct cone is colimiting. -/

140:lemma hom_comp {M N O : ModuleCat.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ O) :

/- Provided for rewriting. -/
lemma id_apply (M : ModuleCat.{v} R) (x : M) :
    (𝟙 M : M ⟶ M) x = x := by simp

@[simp]
lemma hom_comp {M N O : ModuleCat.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ O) :
    (f ≫ g).hom = g.hom.comp f.hom := rfl

/- Provided for rewriting. -/
lemma comp_apply {M N O : ModuleCat.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ O) (x : M) :
    (f ≫ g) x = g (f x) := by simp

@[ext]
lemma hom_ext {M N : ModuleCat.{v} R} {f g : M ⟶ N} (hf : f.hom = g.hom) : f = g :=
  Hom.ext hf

hn : 0 < n
i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2)
R : CommRingCat := Γ(C.left, U)
Z₁ : (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ModuleCat k :=
  fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) (i a))))
Z_int : (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↥s₀) → ModuleCat k :=
  fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) (i a))))
e₁ : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) ≃ₗ[k] (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ↑(Z₁ i) := (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv
e_int : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_int) ≃ₗ[k] (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↥s₀) → ↑(Z_int i) :=
  (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z_int).toLinearEquiv
perI₁ : (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) → Module ↑R ↑(Z₁ i) :=
  fun i ↦ (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ⋯).op)).toModule
h_mod_pi₁ : Module (↑R) ((i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ↑(Z₁ i)) :=
  Pi.module (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) (fun i ↦ ↑(Z₁ i)) ↑R
perI_int : (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↥s₀) → Module ↑R ↑(Z_int i) :=
  fun i ↦ (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ⋯).op)).toModule
h_mod_pi_int : Module (↑R) ((i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↥s₀) → ↑(Z_int i)) :=
  Pi.module (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↥s₀) (fun i ↦ ↑(Z_int i)) ↑R
r : ↑R
y : (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ↑(Z₁ i)
j' : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↥s₀
⊢ (ConcreteCategory.hom
        ((Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦
            Pi.π (fun j ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) (j a)))))
                (i_1 ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (SimplexCategory.δ i))) ≫

                      ((SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (SimplexCategory.δ i)) x)).op) ≫

      (e₁.symm (r • y)) =

                  (Pi.lift fun x ↦
                      Pi.π (fun a ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) (i_1 a))

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc, elementwise]
theorem Pi.lift_π {β : Type w} {f : β → C} [HasProduct f] {P : C} (p : ∀ b, P ⟶ f b) (b : β) :
    Pi.lift p ≫ Pi.π f b = p b := by
  simp only [limit.lift_π, Fan.mk_pt, Fan.mk_π_app]

/-- A version of `Cone.ext` for `Fan`s. -/
@[simps!]

@[reassoc, elementwise]
theorem Pi.lift_π {β : Type w} {f : β → C} [HasProduct f] {P : C} (p : ∀ b, P ⟶ f b) (b : β) :
    Pi.lift p ≫ Pi.π f b = p b := by
  simp only [limit.lift_π, Fan.mk_pt, Fan.mk_π_app]

@[simp] lemma toLinearEquiv_apply (i : X ≅ Y) (x : X) : i.toLinearEquiv x = i.hom x := rfl
@[simp] lemma toLinearEquiv_symm (i : X ≅ Y) : i.toLinearEquiv.symm = i.symm.toLinearEquiv := rfl
@[simp] lemma toLinearMap_toLinearEquiv (i : X ≅ Y) : i.toLinearEquiv = i.hom.hom := rfl

end CategoryTheory.Iso

abbrev RingHom.toModule [Semiring R] [Semiring S] (f : R →+* S) : Module R S :=
  Module.compHom S f

lemma RingHom.toModule_smul [Semiring R] [Semiring S] (f : R →+* S) (x : R) (y : S) :
    letI := f.toModule
    x • y = f x * y :=
  rfl

/-- If the module action of `R` on `S` is compatible with multiplication on `S`, then
`fun x ↦ x • 1` is a ring homomorphism from `R` to `S`.

This is the `RingHom` version of `MonoidHom.smulOneHom`.

When `R` is commutative, usually `algebraMap` should be preferred. -/
@[simps!] def RingHom.smulOneHom
    [Semiring R] [NonAssocSemiring S] [Module R S] [IsScalarTower R S S] : R →+* S where
  __ := MonoidHom.smulOneHom
  map_zero' := zero_smul R 1
  map_add' := (add_smul · · 1)

/-- A homomorphism between semirings R and S can be equivalently specified by an R-module
